description: In a cafeteria, Joey sits at a table across from the Tour Guide, both wearing blue blazers. Joey gestures with his hands as he says, "Anyway, look, I don't know about you and your jackets and your separate tables but Ross is one of my best friends. And if I save him a"
Joey points forward with his right hand and says, "seat, I'm telling you, he will sit in it."
Ross, wearing a white coat and carrying a food tray, walks into the cafeteria.
Joey waves his hand and calls out loudly, "Ross. Ross." Ross stops and looks toward Joey.
Joey gestures to the empty seat beside him and says, "Over here, man. I saved your seat."
Ross pauses, looks at Joey, glances around the room, and then looks back at Joey.
Ross says, "That's okay."
Ross steps backward and says, "I'm cool over here. I'll catch up with you later. Joey,"
Ross walks to a table where others in white coats are seated. A woman in a blue blazer sits in the empty chair next to Joey. The Tour Guide points her fork toward the woman.
The Tour Guide says, "Is this saved?"
The woman in the blue blazer stands up and walks away. Joey looks down at his table.
Joey says, "gift shop."
The scene changes to the exterior of an apartment building at night. A yellow taxi drives past on the street.
Inside an apartment, Joey sits at a kitchen table eating a sandwich. Ross enters through the front door, wearing a dark coat and carrying a briefcase.
Ross walks toward the table, sets down his briefcase, and says, "I'm really, really sorry about what happened in the cafeteria today."
Joey looks at Ross while chewing his food.
Joey raises his hands, palms up, and says, "It's no big deal. You know, you do what you gotta do, right?"
Ross replies, "No, hey, it's not just me. I mean, the scientists and the tour guides"
Joey wraps his sandwich in paper.
Ross says, "never sit together." Joey nods slightly.
Joey turns away from the table.
Joey walks toward the counter and says, "Whatever. It's like that everywhere." Ross steps toward the living area and calls out, "Joey."

Ross approaches the couch where Monica and Rachel are sitting. Ross gestures toward Monica and says, "Okay, Mon, back me up here. Where you work, the waiters eat with the waiters, right? And the chefs eat with the other chefs. Right?"
Monica looks at Ross and says, "I eat by myself in the alley because everybody hates me." Monica makes a small hand gesture, and Rachel raises her hand briefly. Audience laughter is heard.
Ross turns away from Monica. Joey walks back from the kitchen, his arms crossed.
Ross gestures toward Rachel and says, "Okay, Rach, when was the last time you had lunch with the shipping clerks?"
Rachel replies, "Okay, well, I've never actually eaten lunch with the shipping"
Rachel gestures with her hands as she continues, "clerks. But it's totally different. They're not allowed in the executive cafeteria."
Audience laughter is heard as Rachel finishes her sentence.
Rachel points a finger and says, "Oh, I see what you're saying." Audience laughter continues.
Joey continues to stand with his arms crossed, looking at Ross.
Joey uncrosses his arms and says, "Ross,"
Joey gestures with his hands and says, "really, it's no big deal. You wear a white coat. I wear a blue blazer. That means we can't be friends at work. Then so be it. I"
Joey taps his chest and says, "understand."
Ross looks at Joey, maintaining his gaze.
Joey points at Ross and says, "When I'm in a play and you're in the audience, I don't talk to you."
Joey asks, "Right?" Audience laughter is heard.
Ross stares at Joey without changing his expression.
Joey gestures with his right hand and says, "So it's cool. I'll see you tomorrow." Joey walks past Ross toward the door.
Joey exits the apartment. Ross turns to watch him go, then looks back toward the couch.
Monica, sitting on the couch next to Rachel, says, "Yeah. When we're in the audience, he doesn't talk to us, but he does wave." Audience laughter is heard.
The scene changes to a daytime exterior shot of an apartment building. A yellow taxi drives past on the snow-covered street.
Inside the apartment, Phoebe sits on the couch holding a small puppy, while Monica and Rachel sit nearby. Phoebe repeatedly says, "Who's my puppy? Who's my puppy? Who's my puppy?"
Rachel looks at Phoebe and smiles.
Rachel asks, "Have you named him yet?" Phoebe replies, "No, I don't want to get attached."
Rachel reaches toward the puppy.
Rachel asks, "Can I hold him?" Phoebe says, "Sure. Here, come here, squirmy," and hands the puppy to Rachel.
Rachel holds the puppy in her hands.
Rachel says, "Hello." Phoebe abruptly reaches out and says, "Not like that. No,"
Phoebe takes the puppy back from Rachel.
Phoebe says, "wrong."
Phoebe holds the puppy up near her face. Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler walks into the apartment and says, "Hi."
Phoebe hums a tune, holding the puppy up to her ear. Chandler walks toward the kitchen.
Chandler stops, looks toward Phoebe, and asks, "Why is she singing to Karl Malden?" Audience laughter is heard.
Phoebe lowers the puppy. Audience laughter continues.
Phoebe stands up with the puppy and says, "Oh, you know what? I think it's time for puppy to go out again."
Phoebe turns toward the balcony doors and says, "Come on, let's go to the balcony."
Phoebe takes a step toward the balcony, then pauses.

Phoebe abruptly turns toward the front door and says, "What? On the street? Come on, let's go to the street." She opens the door, points a finger back into the room, and says, "Oh, listen, don't go on the balcony until after I get back."
Phoebe exits the apartment and closes the door. Chandler stares in her direction.

In an apartment living area, Monica and Rachel kneel on the floor, leaning their arms on the back of the sofa. Chandler stands in the kitchen area. Rachel asks, "So did you do it?" Chandler nods and says, "Yes. We had the sex." Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler looks forward.
Rachel asks, "It was bad?" Chandler shakes his head and replies, "It was fine." Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler continues, "But she didn't agree with me as strongly as she agreed with Joey." Monica and Rachel rest their chins on their hands.
Chandler turns his head slightly.
Chandler extends one hand forward and says, "She was more like I see your point. I'm all right with it." Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler lowers his hand.
Monica gestures with one hand and says, "It was the first time. There's not always a lot of agreement the first time."
Rachel nods and says, "Yeah, not for girls, anyway. Guys agree like that," snapping her fingers. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler begins to walk forward.
Chandler steps toward the sofa, pointing at the women, and says, "Look, you have to help me, okay?"
Chandler walks around the coffee table, sits down on it opposite the women, and says, "I mean, I know what to do with a woman." Rachel sits down on the sofa.
Chandler shifts his posture.
Chandler gestures with his hands and says, "I know where everything goes. It's always nice."
Chandler lowers his hands.
Chandler leans forward and says, "But I need to know what makes it go from nice to."
Chandler spreads his hands and continues, "my God, somebody's killing her in there," his volume rising slightly. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica looks at Chandler.

Monica says, "All right, I'm going to show you something a lot of guys don't know." She points and adds, "Rach, hand me that pad, please." Rachel picks up a notepad and pen from the sofa and hands them to Monica.
Monica holds out her hand for the pad.
Monica takes the notepad and begins drawing. Chandler raises his hands and says, "All right, now, well, you don't have to draw an actual."
Chandler continues, "whoa, she's hot," his eyes widening on the last phrase. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica continues writing on the notepad.
Monica looks at the pad and says, "Everybody knows the basic erogenous zones."
Monica taps her pen on the paper and adds, "You got one, two, three, four, five, six,"
Monica finishes counting, saying, "and seven. There are seven." Chandler tilts his head and looks at her.
Rachel turns to Monica.
Rachel leans closer to Monica and says, "Let me see that." Monica holds the pad toward her.
Rachel looks at the notepad.
Rachel nods and says, "Yeah."
Monica hands the notepad to Chandler. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler points at the notepad and says, "that's one." Monica replies, "Kind of an important one."
Chandler looks closely at the pad.
Chandler stares at the pad, then looks up and says, "Oh, you know you know what? I was looking at it upside down." Audience laughter is audible.
Monica smiles and Rachel looks toward Chandler.
Rachel raises a hand, touches her hair, and says, "Well, you know, sometimes that helps." Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel tosses her hair back. Monica adjusts the notepad on her lap.
Monica taps the pen on the pad and says, "Okay, now, most guys will hit one, two, three and then go to seven and set up camp."
Chandler looks at Monica.
Chandler asks, "That's bad?" Rachel turns to him and says, "Well, if you go to Disneyland, you don't spend the whole day on the Matterhorn." Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler turns his head slightly.
Chandler replies, "Well, you might if it were anything like seven," nodding slightly.
Rachel looks at Chandler with her lips pressed together. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica holds up a finger and says, "All right, the important thing is to take your time."
Monica continues, "You want to hit them all, and you want to."
Monica continues, "mix them up. You got to keep them on their toes," making a circular motion with her hand. Rachel raises both hands, pointing her index fingers upward, and says, "Toes."
Chandler points a finger upward and says, "Toes."
Rachel lowers her hands behind her head. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel gestures with two fingers near her head and says, "Excuse me."
Rachel turns slightly toward Monica.
Rachel touches her temple and adds, "Yeah, for some people,"
Monica readies her pen over the notepad.
Monica looks at the pad and says, "All right, you could start out with a little one."
Monica moves her pen as if drawing points and says, "A two, a one,"
Monica continues, "two, three."
Monica keeps her pen on the pad.
Monica says, "A three,"
Monica continues moving the pen.
Monica says, "a five,"
Rachel looks at Monica with her lips pressed together.
Monica says, "a four, a three, two," continuing to trace on the pad.
Chandler keeps his gaze fixed on Monica.
Monica rapidly says, "A two, four, six, two, four, six, four, two," her pace quickening as she traces on the pad. Rachel raises a hand near her face.

Monica continues, "two, four, seven, five, seven, six, seven, seven," her volume rising and voice becoming breathy as she closes her eyes and tilts her head back. Rachel also closes her eyes and leans her head back. Audience laughter builds.
Monica loudly repeats, "and seven. Seven," keeping her head tilted back and eyes closed. Audience laughter is loud.
Monica and Rachel keep their eyes closed, their breathing visibly heavy.
Monica slowly lowers her head and opens her eyes. Rachel keeps her eyes closed, her chest rising and falling.
Rachel opens her eyes, raises two fingers near her head, and exhales audibly. Chandler stares at them with his eyes wide. Audience laughter continues.
Monica holds out the notepad toward Chandler and says, "There you are."
Chandler reaches for the pad.
Chandler slowly takes the notepad and says softly, "Yeah, that'll work." Audience laughter continues.
Chandler looks down at the notepad.
In a living room, Monica and Rachel sit on a sofa. Rachel throws her hands up and lowers them. Chandler pushes back from the coffee table and stands.
Chandler picks up a notepad from the table. He turns and walks away.
Chandler walks through a doorway into a bathroom, stopping in front of the sink. He looks down at the notepad in his hand. Audience laughter is audible.
The setting changes to a cafeteria. People in white lab coats and blue blazers sit at separate tables. Joey sits at a central table with other men in blue blazers.
Ross, wearing a white lab coat over a shirt and tie, walks in holding a food tray. He stops and looks around the room.
A man in a white coat at a side table gestures toward an empty chair and says, "Dr. Geller, there's a seat over here." Ross stands holding his tray.
Ross looks at the man and says, "Thank you, Dr."
Ross continues, "Phillips."
Ross lowers his gaze and steps forward.
Ross walks toward the center table where Joey is sitting. He gestures to the table and says, "But I'm having my lunch at this table here in the middle." Joey looks up at him.
Ross continues walking toward the center table.
Ross places his tray on the table, points to Joey, and says, "I'm having my lunch right here with my good friend Joey." Audience laughter is heard. Joey glances around at the other men at the table.
Ross keeps his hand extended toward Joey.
Ross adds, "If he'll sit with me."
Joey holds a sandwich, looks at Ross, and then looks down. He places his sandwich on his tray.
Joey picks up his tray and stands up from his chair. Audience laughter is audible.
Holding his tray, Joey looks at Ross and says, "I will sit with you, Dr. Geller."
Joey walks around the table toward Ross. Ross extends his hand, and they shake hands. The audience cheers and applauds. Joey sets his tray down.
Ross turns to address the rest of the cafeteria, standing at the head of the table. He says, "You know, we work in a museum of natural history."
Ross drops his hands to his sides.
Ross continues, gesturing with his hands, "And yet there is something unnatural".
Ross pauses, looking around the room.
Ross points at Joey, then at his own chest, and says, "about the way we eat lunch. And I want you to know, it took a tour guide to show this scientist the".
Ross finishes the sentence, "error of his ways," gesturing to himself.
Ross lowers his hands and steps forward.
Ross opens his arms widely and says, "You might even say he took me on a tour".
Ross brings both hands to rest on his chest.
Ross says softly, "of myself."
Joey smiles and nods slightly. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey gestures to himself with one hand and says, "Yeah, that was me."
Ross looks back at the room.
Ross sweeps his arm across the room and says, "Now, I look around this cafeteria, and you know what I see? I see division. Division between people in white".
Ross gestures to the tables, saying, "coats and people in blue blazers. And I ask myself, my God, why?" Two men in white coats watch him. Audience laughter is heard.
Ross brings his hands to his chest again.
Ross says, "No, I say we shed these these coats that separate us and we get to know the people". He grabs the lapels of his white coat.
Ross pulls the lapels forward.
Ross says, "underneath," as he begins pulling off his white coat.
Ross pulls his arms out of his white coat and throws it onto the floor behind him. Audience laughter is heard.
Ross spreads his arms and says loudly, "I'm lost."
Ross holds his arms out wide.
Ross continues, "I'm divorced, and I have a kid." He drops his hands to his sides. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross stands with his arms resting at his sides. Joey pushes his chair back and stands up.
Joey unbuttons his blue blazer, pulls it off, and tosses it behind him while looking at Ross. Audience laughter continues.
Joey looks around the room and says loudly, "I'm Joey."
Joey points a thumb at his chest and says, "I'm an actor."
Joey crosses his arms.
Joey shakes his head, keeps his arms crossed into an 'X' over his chest, and says, "I don't know squat about dinosaurs." Ross and Joey shake hands. Audience applause and cheers are heard.
A man wearing a white shirt and tie stands up at another table, throwing his jacket onto the back of his chair.
The man says, "I'm Ted, and I just moved here a month ago."
Ted continues, "And New York really scares me." Joey points at him and says, "All right, there you go. Yeah, you hang in".
Joey finishes, "there, Teddy."
An older man in a blue shirt and tie at another table stands up, taking off his white lab coat.
The older man holds up a piece of fruit and says, "I'm Andrew. And I didn't pay for this pear." Audience laughter is audible.
Ross turns his attention toward Andrew and extends his arm.
Ross points at Andrew and says, "Okay, good for you."

The Tour Guide, Rhonda, wearing a white shirt and tie, stands up and pulls off her blue blazer. She says loudly, "I'm Rhonda. And these aren't real," grabbing her chest. Audience laughter and cheers are heard.
Ross and Joey stare at Rhonda with wide eyes.
Ross and Joey look down toward the floor. Audience laughter continues.
Joey looks back toward Rhonda and says, "Wow, Rhonda."
Ross stares straight ahead.
In a cafeteria, Ross stands near a table while Joey remains seated. A man in a white coat stands up nearby.
The man says, "I'm Scott." Ross raises a fist and replies, "Yeah, okay." Audience laughter is heard.
Ross points toward him and says, "Scott."
The man in the white coat sits down, and a man in a pink shirt stands up.
The man in the pink shirt says, "and I need to flip the light switch on and off 17 times before I leave a room or my family will die." Audience laughter is heard.
Ross stands with his hands at his sides while Joey looks at the man.
A snowy street view shows buildings and a vertical "GARAGE" sign.
Inside a coffee shop, Rachel, Monica, and Phoebe sit on a couch. Phoebe holds a small dog in her lap and says, "My mom's going to be here any minute."
Phoebe continues, "I can't do this. I can't give him oh, yes."
Phoebe says, "No, I can't."
Phoebe lifts the dog slightly.
Phoebe says, "I don't want to, but I can."
Phoebe brings the dog closer to her chest.
Phoebe says, "Nope," followed by audience laughter.
Rachel touches her forehead.
Rachel, rubbing her head, says, "Oh, I can't watch this. It's like Sophie's Choice." Audience laughter is heard.
Monica looks at Rachel.
Monica says, "No one ever saw that." Rachel gestures with her hand and replies, "Oh, it was only okay," followed by audience laughter.
Phoebe strokes the dog.
Phoebe says, "I can't do this. My mom was right."
Phoebe continues, "If I can't give him up and there's no way I can give up a little baby."
Monica looks at Phoebe.
Phoebe says, "Frank and Alice are going to be so crushed."
Phoebe gestures with her hand.
Phoebe asks, "What else can I give them? A kidney?"
Rachel and Monica remain seated on the couch.
Frank Jr. and a woman in a brown coat and red scarf enter. The woman says, "Hi."
Phoebe turns her head toward them.
The woman says, "We were just in the neighborhood."
Frank Jr. steps closer to the couch.
Frank Jr., gesturing, says, "Yeah. So we just thought we'd stop by and let you know that there's still no pressure."
The woman points over her shoulder and says, "None but if there was something you wanted to tell us, we're just going to be right over there having coffee."
The woman turns to leave, then Frank Jr. notices the dog.
Frank Jr. leans toward Phoebe and asks, "Oh, who's this little guy?"
Frank Jr. reaches out, and Phoebe hands the dog to him.
The woman touches the dog and says, "Oh, he's so cute. He reminds me of my old dog, Tumor."
Monica and Phoebe smile while looking at the dog.
The woman holds the dog's paws and says, "You are so precious, I could just take you home."
Phoebe looks at her.
Phoebe asks, "Hey, why don't you?"
Frank Jr. asks, "Are you serious?"
Phoebe nods and replies, "Yeah."
Frank Jr. says, "Oh, thanks," as he and the woman hug the dog. Audience laughter is heard.
Rachel leans toward Phoebe.
Rachel asks, "What are you doing?" Audience laughter is heard.
Phoebe turns to Rachel and says, "No, I'm really okay with this. You know why?"
Phoebe gestures toward Frank Jr. and the woman.
Phoebe says, "Because look at them. Look how happy they are. And I made that, so"
Phoebe smiles and pauses.
Phoebe continues, "I know it's going to be, like, a million times harder to give up a baby,"
Phoebe adds, "but oh, my God, it's going to feel, like, a million times"
Phoebe finishes, "better, right?"
Phoebe places her hands on her chest.
Phoebe stands up and says, "I want to do this. I want to carry your baby." Audience cheers and applause are heard.
Frank Jr. and the woman gasp, and the woman places her hands on her chest. They step forward and embrace Phoebe.
The woman says, "Thank you so much."
The group embrace continues.
The woman says, "You don't know what this means to us."
They step back from the hug.
The woman, hands covering her mouth, says, "Oh, my God, I think I'm going to cry."
Phoebe and Frank Jr. hug.
Frank Jr. says, "Oh, my God."
Frank Jr. holds the dog while hugging Phoebe.
Monica rubs Rachel's arm and says, "It's going to be so great."
Phoebe Sr. walks into the coffee shop wearing a red coat.
Phoebe Sr. says, "Hi. What's going on?"
Phoebe replies, "Oh, I gave them the puppy, and it made them so happy that I decided I'm going to carry their baby."
Phoebe Sr. looks at Phoebe.
Phoebe Sr. says, "Oh, Phoebe." Phoebe begins, "No, I know."
Phoebe continues, "You and I are totally different people, though, and this is a totally different situation,"
Phoebe adds, "and I know that I am not going to regret this."
Phoebe Sr. replies, "I understand all that."
Phoebe Sr. says, "It was just, that was my puppy." Audience laughter is heard.
Phoebe covers her mouth with her hands and says, "Oh," as audience laughter continues.
Phoebe keeps her hands over her mouth.
In a kitchen, Monica sits at a round table holding a pen over papers, while Rachel sits across from her holding an open magazine.
Monica looks toward Rachel and asks, "Would you ever be a surrogate for anyone?" Rachel turns to her and replies, "It depends who asked."
Monica asks, "What if I asked?"
Rachel replies, "Oh, Mon, sure."
Monica asks, "Really?"
Rachel says, "Yes."
Rachel lowers her gaze to her magazine, then looks back up at Monica, who maintains her gaze.
Rachel asks, "You're not asking me, are you?"
Monica shakes her head slightly.
Monica says, "No."
Rachel nods.
Rachel says, "Yes. Totally."
Audience laughter is heard as a woman with short reddish-brown hair, wearing a blue plaid shirt, walks rapidly through the doorway behind them. Her face is scrunched as she approaches Monica.
The woman wraps her arms around Monica from behind and says rapidly, "Thank you. Thank you."
Monica pats the woman's arm.
The woman says, "Thank you."
The woman releases Monica, says, "Yes. Thank you," and walks quickly out the door.
Monica and Rachel look toward the open doorway.
The screen turns black and the audio becomes silent.
The screen remains black in silence.
